element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn to examine objects and interact with NPCs using four basic dialogue topics (Greet, Sarcastic/Joking, Question, Vent Anger)
Four-item pocket limit introduces resource constraint; Luggage provides secondary storage
Players must find specific items across multiple locations to progress story; introduces fetch-quest patterns
Hub-based navigation to different regions becomes available as player learns about new locations
Extra conversation topics unlock based on learned knowledge; encourages exploration and conversation replay
Library access to L-Space allows witnessing past theft and following to brotherhood hideout; significant narrative complexity jump
Acquiring golden items from six city members requires disguise and multi-step problem solving
Player actions (switching summoning book for custard recipe) have story consequences; introduces narrative risk
Requires collecting abstract/comedic items (sword that goes 'ting', birthmark, magic spell) across expanded geography

typical_relief_triggers: NPC dialogue completion revealing new clues
typical_tension_triggers: Location backtracking when solution not found
Dragon encounters escalate in narrative stakes
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation from safe exploration to narrative-driven puzzle chains; acts structure creates distinct tension plateaus; relies heavily on player curiosity and dialogue engagement rather than time pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Extra dialogue topics appear when player learns related information; optional NPC conversations expand world-building

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to point-and-click mechanics and basic item collection; Ankh-Morpork exploration with clear objective (dragon detector parts)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Puzzle complexity increases with brotherhood member infiltration; introduction of time-travel mechanics (L-Space) creates narrative difficulty spike; players must manage multi-step item chains across expanding geography
difficulty_curve.late_game: Highest complexity in abstract hero component assembly and temporal paradox prevention; extended exploration across full Discworld geography; player must synthesize all mechanics and lore learned
notable_spikes: Act 2 transition: L-Space library access and time-travel narrative introduction
Act 2-3 transition: Consequence of summoning book substitution triggers dragon return with no clear solution path
Act 4: Hero component collection requires knowing game-specific lore (sword that goes 'ting' is comedic reference requiring Discworld knowledge or extensive exploration)

notable_level_patterns.description: Overworld map serves as central hub; locations branch into discrete explorable areas within city/regions; players access new spokes as they unlock knowledge of locations
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ankh-Morpork as initial hub; expands to surrounding Disc locations as player progresses

notable_level_patterns.description: Story advancement requires collecting specific items (dragon detector parts, golden control items, hero components); each act introduces new collection objective
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Act 1: Four dragon detector components; Act 2: Six golden items from brotherhood members; Act 4: Hero component set

notable_level_patterns.description: NPCs reveal locations, items, and story information through conversation; players must revisit NPCs with new topics as they gain knowledge; conversation topics unlock contextually
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Throughout all locations; Archchancellor, Dibbler, Librarian serve as info hubs

notable_level_patterns.description: Player choices (switching summoning book) trigger unintended story escalation; introduces branching consequence chains that affect later puzzle requirements
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Act 3: Book substitution leads to dragon re-summoning and new objective in Act 4

sources_used: wikipedia: Discworld (video game) - Gameplay section, Plot summary
